items.title: Match activation to layer role
items.avoid: Defaulting to sigmoid for all layers because it produces nice 0–1 numbers.
items.prefer: ReLU (or variant) in deep hidden layers; sigmoid only on outputs or gates that need (0, 1) bounds.
items.why: Role determines gradient health — hidden layers need strong, non-saturating derivatives; outputs need valid probabilities.
items.example: nn.Sequential(Linear(128,64), ReLU(), Linear(64,1)) + BCEWithLogitsLoss — not Sigmoid() after every Linear().

items.title: Keep pre-activations away from saturation
items.avoid: Training with unscaled features or huge initial weights that push |x|≫0 from step one.
items.prefer: Feature scaling, Xavier/Glorot init for sigmoid-era nets, batch norm or layer norm in deeper stacks.
items.why: σ′(x) peaks at 0.25 only near x≈0; saturated units pass negligible gradients backward.
items.example: StandardScaler on tabular inputs before an MLP; verify pre-activation histogram centered near 0 in TensorBoard.

items.title: Train with logits when using BCE
items.avoid: Computing sigmoid explicitly then feeding probabilities into BCELoss during training.
items.prefer: BCEWithLogitsLoss on raw logits; apply sigmoid only for inference dashboards and thresholding.
items.why: Fused loss is numerically stable and preserves stronger gradients than saturating early.

items.title: Monitor for dead saturated units
items.avoid: Assuming slow convergence is always a learning-rate problem when many σ′ values hover near zero.
items.prefer: Log histograms of pre-activations and σ′; if most mass is at |x|>4, change activation, init, or scaling.
items.why: Saturation is a structural activation issue — no learning-rate tweak fully fixes zero local slope.
items.example: If 80% of hidden pre-activations exceed 4.0 after epoch 1, switch hidden activation from Sigmoid to ReLU.
